ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Abimelɛk sa Eebraham mbolpɔ nɛ ana nɛ anyanyɛn nɛ anyache ŋkeŋ bɛta mbe eche Sɛɛra n ti so n sa mo.
translated: Then Abimelech said to Abraham, "What did you have in view, that you did this thing?"

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Nɛ Jeekɔb kpal loŋ so n nya agbo n wɔtɔ Leeban nseŋ bishi mo: “Manɛ be kulubi nɛ n wora, nɛ fo kpal kumo so n ju a bɛ ma so?
translated: And Jacob said to Laban, "What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served you? Why then have you deceived me?

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: N nyi fo ka shile ŋkpal kenaŋ bɛta n yɔ fo tuto pe be asheŋ ka pere fo kenishi ga so so, ama manɛ e ba nɛ fo naa yuri ma agbir malɛ a shile?”
translated: What is it that makes you think you can just get up and go to the farm without eating, don't you know that if you don't eat you won't have the strength to do the work?

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: Amoso nɛ mee yɔ nɛ n ya keni fanɛ asheŋ nɛ b yɛ baa wora na kikɛ la kashenteŋ nna a. Saŋɛ na so meeŋ pin kusɔ nɛ k wɔtɔ.”
translated: The chief and his people went to the farm and saw that the farm was full of weeds. The chief then said, "Let's clear the weeds and then we will farm."

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Eebraham ka malga loŋ n loge nɛ e shile n yɔ kenalu to n ya pɛ kena fɔlbi danso nɛ e maŋ naŋ shinto m ba sa mbe kenya nɛ e lɔŋɛ mo manaŋ.
translated: Abraham had many children and grandchildren and great-grandchildren, and they all became great nations, and his descendants are still numerous today.
